text: Pre-exercise static stretching reduces maximal strength by approximately 5.4% on average.
Pre-exercise static stretching reduces explosive performance (e.g., jumping, sprinting) by approximately 2.0%.
Pre-exercise static stretching does not produce a statistically significant reduction in power output.
The negative effects of pre-exercise static stretching on performance are not related to age, gender, or fitness level.
Static stretching durations of 45 seconds or less per muscle produce the smallest negative acute effects on performance.
The negative effects of pre-exercise static stretching are more pronounced in isometric tests than in dynamic performance tests.
Static stretching should not be used as the sole warm-up activity before exercise requiring strength or explosive performance.
